channel.title: Follow Builders — X/Twitter
channel.description: AI builders' latest tweets, curated by follow-builders

item.title: Swyx (@swyx): @openclaw @pmarca @latentspacepod we also talk about the need for @alexblania's @worldcoinfnd Proof ...
item.description:

Bio: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
affiliations: - @dxtipshq - @cognition - @temporalio - @aidotengineer - @latentspacepod ❤️ 6 🔁 0

item.title: Swyx (@swyx): @openclaw @pmarca @latentspacepod pls like and subscribe i guess https://t.co/0af86LRpmU
Swyx (@swyx): just learned about create-context-graph - one command and it sets up key entity relationships for 22...
item.description:
just learned about create-context-graph - one command and it sets up key entity relationships for 22 top industry domains. been looking for something like this to "layer on" a social graph to every single app I make! https://t.co/FxBIK0EDrG https://t.co/D8HFstQjjz
Bio: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
affiliations: - @dxtipshq - @cognition - @temporalio - @aidotengineer - @latentspacepod ❤️ 382 🔁 27

item.title:
Peter Yang (@petergyang): The best way to learn to use Claude Cowork is from the designer who helped build it.
Don't miss my ...
item.description:
The best way to learn to use Claude Cowork is from the designer who helped build it.
Don't miss my new episode with Jenny this weekend where she shared:
→ How she uses Cowork at Anthropic → Live demo: From feedback to product deck → The real story behind Cowork's creation
📌 Subscribe to get it on Sunday: https://t.co/Ggqaa3F11Z
Bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 74 🔁 5

item.title:
Peter Yang (@petergyang): Build an /exec-review AI skill to get your leader's product feedback anytime you want.
A simple way...
item.description:
Build an /exec-review AI skill to get your leader's product feedback anytime you want.
A simple way to start is with their decision-making framework — what gets support vs. push back.
Below's a real example from my friend who's a Meta VP. The complete skill has 5 more sections:
→ Core principles → Feedback patterns → Communication style → Review checklist → Example comments
📌 Get the full breakdown here: https://t.co/CyoSh2WEb9
Bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 71 🔁 8

Bio: head of product @linear

item.title: Nan Yu (@thenanyu): What if the next few YC batches have overly correlated performance because they all use GStack, whic...
item.description:
What if the next few YC batches have overly correlated performance because they all use GStack, which causes them to make the same kinds of mistakes but also biases to them towards being selected for admission.
Bio: head of product @linear ❤️ 8 🔁 0

item.title: Nan Yu (@thenanyu): @bennstancil https://t.co/dqAZgjQTrt
Aaron Levie (@levie): The big gap in most enterprises being able to automate work is being able to get right context to th...
item.description:
The big gap in most enterprises being able to automate work is being able to get right context to the agents.
We experience a huge benefit in coding in tech because the problem is generally far simpler than other areas of knowledge work. The codebase contains a bunch of necessary context, access controls and permissions are generally not a major concern, the users are technical enough to supply the context, and the final output is generally quickly verifiable.
Most knowledge work doesn’t look like this. The data is sitting in legacy silos that don’t easily connect to agents, the access controls are all out of whack (people have either too much or too little access), the information isn’t agent-ready, and more.
This is the big context gap for any type of agentic workflows in most organizations right. The platforms that make solving this easy, and the companies that retool their workflows to enable this, will be the winners in a world of agents.
Bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI ❤️ 118 🔁 17

item.title: Garry Tan (@garrytan): I have to say this interview changed my life. Hearing how Boris thinks about software spurred me to ...
item.description:
I have to say this interview changed my life. Hearing how Boris thinks about software spurred me to work much harder on releasing my own way of doing things and on iterating fast on how I build. Hard to believe it has only been a month since this one. https://t.co/y0y5JmQodL
Bio: President & CEO @ycombinator —Founder https://t.co/7aoJjp1iIK—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop—haters not allowed in my sauna ❤️ 63 🔁 5

item.title:
Peter Steinberger (@steipete): Another sick upcoming feature:
/acp spawn codex --bind here LOOK AT ME, I AM CODEX NOW
You could b...
item.description:
Another sick upcoming feature:
/acp spawn codex --bind here LOOK AT ME, I AM CODEX NOW
You could bind codex/claude code/opencode already in threads, now you can take over your current session as well.
Bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world @openclaw🦞 ❤️ 543 🔁 30

item.title: Peter Steinberger (@steipete): The next version of OpenClaw is also an MCP, you can use it instead of Anthropic's message channel M...
item.description:
The next version of OpenClaw is also an MCP, you can use it instead of Anthropic's message channel MCP to connect to a much wider range of message providers.
(I know, this is awkward)
Bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world @openclaw🦞 ❤️ 1548 🔁 69

item.title:
Aditya Agarwal (@adityaag): Everyone wants to avoid taking on the models head on.
Surprising how few people take the opposite a...
item.description:
Everyone wants to avoid taking on the models head on.
Surprising how few people take the opposite approach
Lean into the risk. But make a bet on capabilities that are 12-18 months out. The models suck at something today but I am going to bet it is good enough after XXX months
Bio: General Partner @SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder @Bevel_Health | Ex: Early Eng @facebook, CTO @Dropbox, Board @Flipkart | Optimist, Builder, Dad ❤️ 62 🔁 6
